items.title: Watering the oval is costing this public school more than $100k a year

children.content: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

thumbnail.altText: The entrance at Dunsborough Primary School

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: The man's
permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

image.altText: The silhouette of a woman sitting, bending over with head resting on clasped hands

items.title: Fixing WA's three-bin problem 'not rocket science' say locals, leaders

children.content: With the three-bin FOGO system on shaky ground outside Perth, some locals are urging the WA government to step up and fund a regional processing plant.

image.altText: Piles of dirt with bits of rubbish throughout

items.title: Bones found on Rottnest Island are Indigenous remains

children.content: Several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island this week are historic Indigenous remains, WA Police say.

gallery.altText: Police holding guard around a fence

thumbnail.altText: Police holding guard around a fence

items.title: Could a buried car be the key to a 35-year-old hit and run mystery?

children.content: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

image.altText: A wide shot showing a cross

items.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in gripping finish

children.content: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

image.altText: Matthew Johnson celebrates a goal for the Dockers against the Magpies.

items.title: Man who killed foster brother to stay in jail due to lack of mental facilities

children.content: Ian Brooke was found not guilty of murder due to his mental impairment, but will have to stay in jail because of a lack of suitable mental health care facilities to house him.

gallery.altText: A cul-de-sac is cordoned off by police

thumbnail.altText: A cul-de-sac is cordoned off by police

items.title: Warning system made WA community feel 'unsafe' during ex-Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Residents in the Pilbara raise concerns over the state's warning system for cyclones following ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle last month.

items.title: Convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales

children.content: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

image.altText: A collection of illegally sold vapes

items.title: Alcoa rejects mercury emissions concerns from its WA refinery

children.content: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

image.altText: A large concrete office building with glass windows on a cloudy day, with Alcoa written in large letters.

items.title: WA Police launch probe after video shows officer shoving woman to ground during arrest

children.content: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

items.title: AC Milan, Inter and Juventus to play matches in Perth

children.content: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

image.altText: Opposing players compete for the soccer ball, while another player loiters in support

items.title: Aussie duo suffer shock upsets as Britain takes control of BJK Cup tie

children.content: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

image.altText: Female tennis player stretches out to play a forehand in a match

items.title: Unusable road may be the end of remote community as food supplies dwindle

children.content: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

items.title: This couple has a fixed price contract to build their home — it's gone up $120k

children.content: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

items.title: ASX 200 and Aussie dollar down, Brent crude and WTI oil up — as it happened

children.content: The Australian market has finished the trading week on a disappointing note, dropping slightly, despite US stocks rising overnight. Look back on our business blog.

items.title: 'Not sustainable': Food charities face tough calls as demand skyrockets

children.content: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are being forced to modify their services to meet a surge in demand as more people grapple with rising costs.

items.title: Fights, sex, deaths and glitter: The rocky road to Euphoria season three

children.content: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

items.title: Top neuroscientist slams AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

image.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field

items.title: House empty more than a year after 69-year-old renter forced out

children.content: Following a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is battling for a place to call home — with a Geraldton house she used to rent left sitting unoccupied for over a year after it was bought by state housing.

items.title: Sister pushes to raise awareness of male eating disorders after tragic loss

children.content: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men are at risk.

items.title: Super members blindsided by change in fund terms and conditions

children.content: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

items.title: Sexual misconduct now publicly listed on 100 health practitioners' records

children.content: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

items.title: Travelling to Europe? New biometric border rules start today

children.content: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.
